skills.name: Selectors
skills.desc: Selectors in CSS are patterns used to select and style HTML elements. They can target elements by type, class, ID, attributes, and more.

skills.name: Properties
skills.desc: Properties define the visual characteristics of elements. They include attributes like 'color', 'font-size', 'margin', and 'padding'.

skills.name: Values
skills.desc: Values are assigned to properties to specify how elements should be styled. Examples include 'red' for color and '20px' for size.

skills.name: Colors
skills.desc: CSS offers various ways to specify colors, including named colors, hexadecimal values, RGB, and HSL color models.

skills.name: Fonts
skills.desc: Font properties control the typeface, size, and style of text. Web fonts and system fonts are commonly used.

skills.name: Text Styling
skills.desc: Text styling properties like 'text-align', 'text-decoration', and 'line-height' affect the appearance of text content.

skills.name: Box Model
skills.desc: The box model describes how elements are structured, including content, padding, border, and margin.

skills.name: Margin and Padding
skills.desc: Margin and padding properties control spacing around elements, allowing for separation between elements.

skills.name: Borders
skills.desc: Border properties define the border around elements, specifying its style, width, and color.

skills.name: Width and Height
skills.desc: Width and height properties determine the dimensions of elements, such as 'width: 100px' or 'height: auto'.

skills.name: Display Property
skills.desc: The 'display' property dictates how elements are rendered, including block, inline, and inline-block.

skills.name: Positioning
skills.desc: Positioning properties like 'position' and 'float' influence the placement of elements within the layout.

skills.name: Flexbox
skills.desc: Flexbox is a CSS layout model for creating flexible and responsive designs by distributing space among elements in a container.

skills.name: Grid Layout
skills.desc: Grid Layout allows for the creation of two-dimensional grid-based layouts, making it easier to create complex designs.

skills.name: Backgrounds
skills.desc: Background properties enable the styling of element backgrounds, such as setting colors, images, and gradients.

skills.name: Lists and Tables
skills.desc: CSS can be used to style lists and tables, modifying their appearance and layout.

skills.name: Pseudo-classes
skills.desc: Pseudo-classes like ':hover' and ':nth-child' allow for dynamic styling based on user interaction and element positions.

skills.name: Pseudo-elements
skills.desc: Pseudo-elements like '::before' and '::after' create virtual elements for additional styling and content.

skills.name: Media Queries
skills.desc: Media queries allow CSS rules to be applied based on the characteristics of the user's device, such as screen size.

skills.name: Animations
skills.desc: CSS animations enable the creation of smooth and visually appealing animated effects, like transitions, keyframes, and transforms.

skills.name: Transitions
skills.desc: Transitions specify how CSS property changes should be animated, such as smooth transitions between hover and normal states.

skills.name: Transformations
skills.desc: Transformations allow for 2D and 3D transformations of elements, including translations, rotations, scaling, and skewing.

skills.name: Variables
skills.desc: CSS preprocessors allow the use of variables to store and reuse values, making stylesheets more maintainable and customizable.

skills.name: Import and Extend
skills.desc: Importing and extending stylesheets and rules from one file to another helps manage and modularize CSS code.

skills.name: Comments
skills.desc: Adding comments to CSS code helps document styles, clarify intent, and make it more understandable for developers.
